release.name: v0.4.0
release.body:
## What's Changed

## ✨ Features

* Add agentic workflow daily-repo-status (#163) @tryy3
* feat: redesign agent report outbox and expose runtime outbox settings (#157) @tryy3
* Init agentic workflow (#160) @tryy3

## 🔧 Maintenance

* chore: workflow updates (#180) @tryy3
* Update dependency vue-tsc to v3.2.7 (#174) @[renovate[bot]](https://github.com/apps/renovate)
* chore(ci): stabilize weekly dependency advisory workflow execution (#173) @tryy3
* ci: add issue triage quality and labeling workflow (#172) @tryy3
* chore(ci): fix dependency advisory workflow handling for npm non-zero exits (#171) @tryy3
* chore(ci): add weekly AGENTS.md maintenance workflow (#170) @tryy3
* chore(ci): add weekly dependency risk advisory workflow (#166) @tryy3
* Update github/gh-aw-actions action to v0.68.7 (#162) @[renovate[bot]](https://github.com/apps/renovate)
* chore(ci): remove redundant workflow path filters (#165) @tryy3
* Update actions/setup-python action to v6 (#153) @[renovate[bot]](https://github.com/apps/renovate)
* Update dorny/paths-filter action to v4 (#154) @[renovate[bot]](https://github.com/apps/renovate)
* ci: limit push-triggered CI to main (#151) @tryy3
* Improve agent docs and add Dependabot skill references (#150) @tryy3
* Update actions/checkout action to v6 (#146) @[renovate[bot]](https://github.com/apps/renovate)
* Update actions/labeler action to v6 (#149) @[renovate[bot]](https://github.com/apps/renovate)
* Update actions/github-script action to v9 (#148) @[renovate[bot]](https://github.com/apps/renovate)
* Update actions/checkout action to v5 (#145) @[renovate[bot]](https://github.com/apps/renovate)
* ci: add path-based change detection to skip unaffected CI jobs (#147) @tryy3
* Add release workflow governance, templates, and automation (#144) @tryy3

---

**Full Changelog**: v0.3.0...v0.4.0

prs.title: Add release workflow governance, templates, and automation
prs.whatThisPrDoes:
PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): add release workflow governance and PR automation

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
Release and PR governance was fragmented. Required labels were not consistently enforced, release drafting automation was missing, contributor workflow documentation was sparse, and helper tooling for release notes/polish was not standardized.

After this PR:
The repository has a structured release/PR workflow with:
- PR template updates aligned with labeling and release-note expectations
- Automated PR labeling/title hints and label compliance checks
- Release drafter configuration and refresh workflows
- Updated issue templates and contribution documentation
- Supporting docs for maintainers/release strategy
- Helper scripts and just targets for release-related operations

Fixes #94, fixes #125
prs.body:
### What this PR does

PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): add release workflow governance and PR automation

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
Release and PR governance was fragmented. Required labels were not consistently enforced, release drafting automation was missing, contributor workflow documentation was sparse, and helper tooling for release notes/polish was not standardized.

After this PR:
The repository has a structured release/PR workflow with:
- PR template updates aligned with labeling and release-note expectations
- Automated PR labeling/title hints and label compliance checks
- Release drafter configuration and refresh workflows
- Updated issue templates and contribution documentation
- Supporting docs for maintainers/release strategy
- Helper scripts and just targets for release-related operations

Fixes #94, fixes #125

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

This change establishes a consistent, documented, and partially automated release workflow to reduce manual errors and improve PR quality/release predictability.

The following tradeoffs were made:
- Added multiple workflow/config files to enforce process, increasing CI/workflow surface area.
- Chose explicit documentation and policy files over implicit team conventions.
- Included scripts to support release tasks, which adds maintenance overhead but improves repeatability.

The following alternatives were considered:
- Keeping release workflow as ad-hoc maintainer steps.
- Enforcing conventions solely via docs without CI checks.
- Using fewer automation hooks and relying on PR reviewer discipline.

Links to places where the discussion took place:
N/A

### Breaking changes

If this PR introduces breaking changes, please describe the changes and the impact on users.

No API/runtime breaking changes are introduced. Process/policy expectations for PR metadata and labels are tightened through workflow additions.

### Special notes for your reviewer

- This PR is primarily process/CI/docs oriented and touches many repository meta files.
- Reviewer focus should be on correctness of label taxonomy, workflow triggers, and template consistency.
- Some behavior depends on repository settings/permissions for Actions and labels.

### Labels

Please ensure this PR has:
- exactly one `type/*` label
- one or more `area/*` labels when relevant
- optional `impact/*` labels when they help release context

Type label guidance:
- choose the label that best matches the main reason this PR exists
- if a feature PR also includes docs/tests/refactors, keep `type/feature`
- if the main goal is efficiency, prefer `type/performance` over `type/refactor`

Reference: [docs/release-workflow-plan.md](docs/release-workflow-plan.md)

### Checklist

This checklist is not enforcing, but it's a reminder of items that could be relevant to every PR.
Approvers are expected to review this list.

- [x] PR: The PR description is expressive enough and will help future contributors
- [x] Code: [Write code that humans can understand](https://en.wikiquote.org/wiki/Martin_Fowler#code-for-humans) and [Keep it simple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KISS_principle)
- [x] Refactor: You have [left the code cleaner than you found it (Boy Scout Rule)](https://learning.oreilly.com/library/view/97-things-every/9780596809515/ch08.html)
- [x] Upgrade: Impact of this change on upgrade flows was considered and addressed if required
- [x] Documentation: A [user-guide update](https://docs.cherry-ai.com) was considered and is present (link) or not required. Check this only when the PR introduces or changes a user-facing feature or behavior.
- [x] Self-review: I have reviewed my own code (e.g., via [`/gh-pr-review`](/.claude/skills/gh-pr-review/SKILL.md), `gh pr diff`, or GitHub UI) before requesting review from others
- [x] Labels: This PR has exactly one `type/*` label and appropriate `area/*` labels

### Release note

```release-note
NONE
```

prs.title: ci: add path-based change detection to skip unaffected CI jobs
prs.whatThisPrDoes:
PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): add path-based change detection for jobs

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- CI jobs ran broadly on pull requests even when unrelated parts of the repository changed.
- This increased CI runtime and resource usage for small or scoped changes.

After this PR:
- A new Detect changes job computes path-based change flags.
- Server, agent, frontend, Go fmt/vet, build, lint, and proto jobs are conditionally executed on pull requests only when relevant paths changed.
- Non-pull request events continue to run the full pipeline as before.

Fixes #
N/A
prs.body:
### What this PR does

PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): add path-based change detection for jobs

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- CI jobs ran broadly on pull requests even when unrelated parts of the repository changed.
- This increased CI runtime and resource usage for small or scoped changes.

After this PR:
- A new Detect changes job computes path-based change flags.
- Server, agent, frontend, Go fmt/vet, build, lint, and proto jobs are conditionally executed on pull requests only when relevant paths changed.
- Non-pull request events continue to run the full pipeline as before.

Fixes #
N/A

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The following tradeoffs were made:
- Added a dedicated upfront job and job dependencies to reduce unnecessary downstream job executions on pull requests.
- Included shared trigger paths like proto, workflow files, justfile, and golangci config to avoid under-triggering critical checks.

The following alternatives were considered:
- Keep running all jobs on every pull request for simplicity, but that keeps CI cost/time high.
- Use per-job inlined path checks only, but centralizing change detection is easier to maintain and reason about.

Links to places where the discussion took place: N/A

### Breaking changes

If this PR introduces breaking changes, please describe the changes and the impact on users.

None.

### Special notes for your reviewer

- This change only affects CI workflow execution conditions.
- Workflow logic was updated in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.

### Labels

Please ensure this PR has:

- exactly one `type/*` label
- one or more `area/*` labels when relevant
- optional `impact/*` labels when they help release context

Type label guidance:

- choose the label that best matches the main reason this PR exists
- if a feature PR also includes docs/tests/refactors, keep `type/feature`
- if the main goal is efficiency, prefer `type/performance` over `type/refactor`

Reference: [docs/release-workflow-plan.md](docs/release-workflow-plan.md)

### Checklist

prs.title: Improve agent docs and add Dependabot skill references
prs.releaseNote: Improve contributor and coding-agent documentation by adding a root AGENTS.md, updating CLAUDE.md with CI/PR and release workflow guidance, and adding Dependabot skill references/examples for repository automation guidance.
prs.whatThisPrDoes:
PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: docs: improve agent guidance and add dependabot skill references

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- Agent-facing contribution guidance was split across multiple places, with no root AGENTS.md.
- CLAUDE.md did not include the latest validated CI label policy and release-note workflow details.
- Dependabot skill references/examples were not consolidated in this branch target.

After this PR:
- Added root AGENTS.md with actionable setup, dev, test, build, CI, PR, and troubleshooting instructions for coding agents.
- Updated CLAUDE.md with CI/PR policy, release workflow notes, local dev environment behavior, and troubleshooting additions.
- Added Dependabot skill reference material and examples under .github/skills/dependabot for agent guidance coverage.

Fixes #
N/A
prs.body:
### What this PR does

PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: docs: improve agent guidance and add dependabot skill references

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- Agent-facing contribution guidance was split across multiple places, with no root AGENTS.md.
- CLAUDE.md did not include the latest validated CI label policy and release-note workflow details.
- Dependabot skill references/examples were not consolidated in this branch target.

After this PR:
- Added root AGENTS.md with actionable setup, dev, test, build, CI, PR, and troubleshooting instructions for coding agents.
- Updated CLAUDE.md with CI/PR policy, release workflow notes, local dev environment behavior, and troubleshooting additions.
- Added Dependabot skill reference material and examples under .github/skills/dependabot for agent guidance coverage.

Fixes #
N/A

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The following tradeoffs were made:
- Chose documentation-first updates instead of behavior/code changes to improve contributor and agent effectiveness with low risk.
- Duplicated selected workflow guidance between AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md for discoverability by different tools.

The following alternatives were considered:
- Keeping guidance only in README or workflow docs; rejected because agent tools typically look for AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md first.
- Deferring updates until later; rejected because PR/release-note and label requirements are enforced and should be easy to find now.

Links to places where the discussion took place: N/A

prs.title: ci: limit push-triggered CI to main
prs.whatThisPrDoes:
PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: ci: limit push-triggered CI to main

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- CI workflow push trigger matched all branches with branches: ["**"] in [.github/workflows/ci.yml](.github/workflows/ci.yml#L5), which ran push CI for every branch push.

After this PR:
- CI workflow push trigger is limited to main with branches: ["main"] in [.github/workflows/ci.yml](.github/workflows/ci.yml#L5), while pull_request triggers remain unchanged.

Fixes # N/A
prs.body:
### What this PR does

PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: ci: limit push-triggered CI to main

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- CI workflow push trigger matched all branches with branches: ["**"] in [.github/workflows/ci.yml](.github/workflows/ci.yml#L5), which ran push CI for every branch push.

After this PR:
- CI workflow push trigger is limited to main with branches: ["main"] in [.github/workflows/ci.yml](.github/workflows/ci.yml#L5), while pull_request triggers remain unchanged.

Fixes # N/A

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The following tradeoffs were made:
- Pros: Reduces CI noise and resource usage from branch pushes when PR checks already cover validation.
- Cons: Non-PR branch pushes no longer run push-triggered CI.

The following alternatives were considered:
- Keep branches: ["**"] and accept broader CI execution.
- Add more granular branch patterns, which adds maintenance overhead without clear benefit for this change.

Links to places where the discussion took place: N/A

### Breaking changes

If this PR introduces breaking changes, please describe the changes and the impact on users.
- NONE

### Special notes for your reviewer

- This is a single-line workflow trigger change in [.github/workflows/ci.yml](.github/workflows/ci.yml#L5).

### Labels

Please ensure this PR has:

- exactly one `type/*` label
- one or more `area/*` labels when relevant
- optional `impact/*` labels when they help release context

Type label guidance:

- choose the label that best matches the main reason this PR exists
- if a feature PR also includes docs/tests/refactors, keep `type/feature`
- if the main goal is efficiency, prefer `type/performance` over `type/refactor`

Reference: [docs/release-workflow-plan.md](docs/release-workflow-plan.md)

### Checklist

Approvers are expected to review this list.

prs.title: feat: redesign agent report outbox and expose runtime outbox settings
prs.releaseNote: Added configurable agent outbox delivery settings, including global defaults in Settings and per-agent overrides in the agent detail view. The agent now uses the redesigned outbox path for report delivery with runtime-pushed outbox tuning from the server.
prs.whatThisPrDoes:
PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: feat: redesign agent report outbox and expose runtime outbox settings

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:

- Agent report delivery relied on the old reporter/local_jobs path.
- Outbox behavior was not fully consolidated behind a dedicated two-tier queue.
- The new outbox tunables were not manageable from the frontend using the existing settings pattern.

After this PR:

- Agent report delivery uses a dedicated outbox with in-memory buffering plus SQLite spillover.
- The server can push global outbox settings and per-agent outbox overrides to agents at runtime.
- The frontend exposes both global outbox settings and per-agent override controls.
- The redesign and schema/config behavior are documented.



Fixes #

Related to #33, #104, #109
prs.body:
### What this PR does

PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: feat: redesign agent report outbox and expose runtime outbox settings

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:

- Agent report delivery relied on the old reporter/local_jobs path.
- Outbox behavior was not fully consolidated behind a dedicated two-tier queue.
- The new outbox tunables were not manageable from the frontend using the existing settings pattern.

After this PR:

- Agent report delivery uses a dedicated outbox with in-memory buffering plus SQLite spillover.
- The server can push global outbox settings and per-agent outbox overrides to agents at runtime.
- The frontend exposes both global outbox settings and per-agent override controls.
- The redesign and schema/config behavior are documented.

<!-- (optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*: -->

Fixes #

Related to #33, #104, #109

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The following tradeoffs were made:

- `OUTBOX_MEMORY_MAX` remains a bootstrap-only env var because Go channel capacity cannot be resized safely at runtime.
- The other outbox tunables follow the project’s established settings model: global values in server settings plus optional per-agent JSON overrides pushed through `AgentConfig`.
- The frontend mirrors the existing command-timeout UX rather than introducing a separate settings pattern.

The following alternatives were considered:

- Keeping all outbox tuning entirely environment-variable based on the agent.
- Making all outbox settings global-only without per-agent overrides.
- Resizing the in-memory queue dynamically at runtime.

Links to places where the discussion took place: #33, #104, #109

### Breaking changes

<!-- optional -->

This PR does not introduce a user-facing breaking API change. Existing agent behavior changes internally from the legacy reporter/local_jobs path to the outbox-based delivery path.

### Special notes for your reviewer

<!-- optional -->

- This PR spans agent, server, proto, frontend, and docs because the outbox configuration is now end-to-end.
- Validation completed with `just test`, `just test-frontend`, and frontend type-checking.

### Labels

Please ensure this PR has:

- exactly one `type/*` label
- one or more `area/*` labels when relevant
- optional `impact/*` labels when they help release context

Type label guidance:

- choose the label that best matches the main reason this PR exists
- if a feature PR also includes docs/tests/refactors, keep `type/feature`
- if the main goal is efficiency, prefer `type/performance` over `type/refactor`

Reference: [docs/release-workflow-plan.md](docs/release-workflow-plan.md)

### Checklist

This checklist is not enforcing, but it's a reminder of items that could be relevant to every PR.
Approvers are expected to review this list.

- [x] PR: The PR description is expressive enough and will help future contributors
- [x] Code: [Write code that humans can understand](https://en.wikiquote.org/wiki/Martin_Fowler#code-for-humans) and [Keep it simple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KISS_principle)
- [x] Refactor: You have [left the code cleaner than you found it (Boy Scout Rule)](https://learning.oreilly.com/library/view/97-things-every/9780596809515/ch08.html)
- [x] Upgrade: Impact of this change on upgrade flows was considered and addressed if required
- [x] Documentation: A [user-guide update](https://docs.cherry-ai.com) was considered and is present (link) or not required. Check this only when the PR introduces or changes a user-facing feature or behavior.
- [x] Self-review: I have reviewed my own code (e.g., via [`/gh-pr-review`](/.claude/skills/gh-pr-review/SKILL.md), `gh pr diff`, or GitHub UI) before requesting review from others
- [x] Labels: This PR has exactly one `type/*` label and appropriate `area/*` labels

### Release note

<!--  Write your release note:
1. Enter your extended release note in the below block. If the PR requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the string "action required".
2. If no release note is required, just write "NONE".
3. Only include user-facing changes (new features, bug fixes visible to users, UI changes, behavior changes). For CI, maintenance, internal refactoring, build tooling, or other non-user-facing work, write "NONE".
-->

```release-note
Added configurable agent outbox delivery settings, including global defaults in Settings and per-agent overrides in the agent detail view. The agent now uses the redesigned outbox path for report delivery with runtime-pushed outbox tuning from the server.
```

prs.body:
### What this PR does

PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): remove redundant workflow path filters

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
Workflow path entries were duplicated across multiple component change-detection filters in CI.

After this PR:
Redundant `.github/workflows/**` entries are removed from component-specific filters to avoid unnecessary job fan-out on workflow-only changes.

<!-- (optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*: -->

Fixes #

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The following tradeoffs were made:
The workflow relies on dedicated workflow-change handling instead of repeating workflow paths under each component filter, which reduces duplicate triggers while preserving intended CI coverage.

The following alternatives were considered:
Keeping the duplicated workflow path entries was considered, but it keeps over-triggering jobs that are unrelated to workflow-only changes.

prs.title: chore(ci): add weekly dependency risk advisory workflow
prs.whatThisPrDoes:
PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): add weekly dependency risk advisory workflow

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- There was no scheduled agentic workflow to gather deterministic dependency evidence and produce a ranked weekly advisory for this monorepo.

After this PR:
- Adds a new weekly GitHub Agentic Workflow that:
  - Collects deterministic dependency data for `server`, `agent`, and `frontend`.
  - Writes required raw/readable reports under `reports/`.
  - Uploads report artifacts with 90-day retention.
  - Produces ranked recommendations and updates a single advisory tracking issue.



Fixes #N/A
prs.body:
### What this PR does

PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): add weekly dependency risk advisory workflow

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- There was no scheduled agentic workflow to gather deterministic dependency evidence and produce a ranked weekly advisory for this monorepo.

After this PR:
- Adds a new weekly GitHub Agentic Workflow that:
  - Collects deterministic dependency data for `server`, `agent`, and `frontend`.
  - Writes required raw/readable reports under `reports/`.
  - Uploads report artifacts with 90-day retention.
  - Produces ranked recommendations and updates a single advisory tracking issue.

<!-- (optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*: -->

Fixes #N/A

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

This introduces a repeatable dependency risk process that combines deterministic evidence and agentic prioritization so maintainers can make informed upgrade/removal decisions with auditable artifacts.

The following tradeoffs were made:
- The compiled lock file is large, but required for gh-aw execution.
- Deterministic collection continues on partial failures to preserve visibility and avoid all-or-nothing runs.

The following alternatives were considered:
- Deterministic-only reporting without ranking: rejected because prioritization stays manual.
- Agentic-only reporting without artifact evidence: rejected because it reduces verifiability.

Links to places where the discussion took place: N/A

### Breaking changes

<!-- optional -->

If this PR introduces breaking changes, please describe the changes and the impact on users.

None.

### Special notes for your reviewer

- Compiled and validated with:
  - `gh aw compile --strict weekly-dependency-risk-and-upgrade-advisory`
- New files:
  - `.github/workflows/weekly-dependency-risk-and-upgrade-advisory.md`
  - `.github/workflows/weekly-dependency-risk-and-upgrade-advisory.lock.yml`

prs.title: chore(ci): add weekly AGENTS.md maintenance workflow
prs.whatThisPrDoes:
PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): add weekly AGENTS.md maintenance workflow

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- AGENTS.md updates depended on manual maintenance and could drift from repository reality over time.

After this PR:
- Adds an agentic workflow that runs weekly to review merged PRs and changed files, update AGENTS.md when needed, and open a maintenance PR.
- Adds compiled lock workflow generated by gh-aw.
- Adds gh-aw action pin lock file used by compiler-managed action pinning.

Fixes #
prs.body:
### What this PR does

PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): add weekly AGENTS.md maintenance workflow

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- AGENTS.md updates depended on manual maintenance and could drift from repository reality over time.

After this PR:
- Adds an agentic workflow that runs weekly to review merged PRs and changed files, update AGENTS.md when needed, and open a maintenance PR.
- Adds compiled lock workflow generated by gh-aw.
- Adds gh-aw action pin lock file used by compiler-managed action pinning.

Fixes #

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

This introduces a low-noise maintenance automation to keep AGENTS.md accurate without requiring manual recurring work. The workflow is schedule-driven, uses safe outputs for PR creation, and uses cache-memory to track the previous run context.

The following tradeoffs were made:
- Added generated workflow artifacts (`.lock.yml` and `.github/aw/actions-lock.json`) to keep compilation deterministic and auditable.
- Kept scope to workflow infrastructure only (no direct content rewrite of AGENTS.md in this PR).

The following alternatives were considered:
- Manual AGENTS.md updates only (rejected: higher risk of stale agent instructions).
- Updating AGENTS.md in-place without PRs (rejected: reduces review/auditability).

Links to places where the discussion took place: None

### Breaking changes

None.

If this PR introduces breaking changes, please describe the changes and the impact on users.

### Special notes for your reviewer

- This is CI/workflow automation only.
- The workflow creates PRs via `safe-outputs.create-pull-request` when AGENTS.md changes are needed.
- If no update is required, it emits `noop`.

### Labels

Please ensure this PR has:

- exactly one `type/*` label
- one or more `area/*` labels when relevant
- optional `impact/*` labels when they help release context

- choose the label that best matches the main reason this PR exists

- if the main goal is efficiency, prefer `type/performance` over `type/refactor`

prs.title: chore(ci): fix dependency advisory workflow handling for npm non-zero exits
prs.whatThisPrDoes:
- Pins weekly dependency advisory workflow Go runtime to `1.26.1`.
- Fixes npm evidence collection handling so expected non-zero exit codes from `npm outdated --json` and `npm audit --json` are treated as valid signal, not hard failures.
- Regenerates the compiled lock workflow to match source changes.

Fixes # N/A
prs.body:
### What this PR does

- Pins weekly dependency advisory workflow Go runtime to `1.26.1`.
- Fixes npm evidence collection handling so expected non-zero exit codes from `npm outdated --json` and `npm audit --json` are treated as valid signal, not hard failures.
- Regenerates the compiled lock workflow to match source changes.

Fixes # N/A

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

- Previous runs reported npm evidence collection failures even when commands returned meaningful JSON with exit code `1`.
- Added a targeted helper (`run_json_allow_rc`) rather than broad `|| true` so failure semantics stay strict for other commands.
- Go runtime is pinned to match `go.mod` minimum (`1.26.1`) to avoid toolchain mismatch in evidence steps.

### Breaking changes

N/A

### Special notes for your reviewer

- This is CI/workflow behavior only; no app runtime logic changes.
- Lock-file updates are generated and expected.

### Checklist

- [x] PR description is clear
- [x] Self-review completed
- [x] Labels set appropriately

### Release note

```release-note
NONE
```

prs.title: ci: add issue triage quality and labeling workflow
prs.whatThisPrDoes:
**Suggested squash subject:** `ci: add issue triage quality and labeling workflow`

**Before this PR:** 
Issues are triaged manually by maintainers, which can be inconsistent and time-consuming. Issues may have unclear titles, missing descriptions, incorrect labels, or duplicates go undetected.

**After this PR:**
A new GitHub Agentic Workflow (`issue-triage-quality-and-labeling`) continuously improves issue metadata and clarity:
- Automatically applies correct labels based on issue type, component, and impact.
- Detects and reports potential duplicates.
- Flags quality gaps (missing reproduction steps, unclear titles, missing environment info).
- Asks concise clarifying questions for underspecified issues.
- Recommends moving questions/discussions to the Discussions section per repository policy.
- For frontend UX/accessibility issues, asks for WCAG AA and usability-relevant details.

The workflow is conservative: it avoids label churn and only posts a triage comment when action is needed.

**Fixes:** N/A
prs.body:
### What this PR does

**Suggested squash subject:** `ci: add issue triage quality and labeling workflow`

**Before this PR:** 
Issues are triaged manually by maintainers, which can be inconsistent and time-consuming. Issues may have unclear titles, missing descriptions, incorrect labels, or duplicates go undetected.

**After this PR:**
A new GitHub Agentic Workflow (`issue-triage-quality-and-labeling`) continuously improves issue metadata and clarity:
- Automatically applies correct labels based on issue type, component, and impact.
- Detects and reports potential duplicates.
- Flags quality gaps (missing reproduction steps, unclear titles, missing environment info).
- Asks concise clarifying questions for underspecified issues.
- Recommends moving questions/discussions to the Discussions section per repository policy.
- For frontend UX/accessibility issues, asks for WCAG AA and usability-relevant details.

The workflow is conservative: it avoids label churn and only posts a triage comment when action is needed.

**Fixes:** N/A

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

**Rationale:**
- Consistent labeling improves release planning and issue prioritization.
- Early duplicate detection saves maintainer time and improves user experience.
- Guiding issue quality upfront (title clarity, description completeness) makes future triage and implementation faster.
- Repository policies are enforced consistently across all issue types.
- Conservative noise-reduction approach keeps maintainers focused on signal, not bot spam.

**Tradeoffs made:**
- The workflow does NOT auto-close duplicates (requires maintainer judgment).
- The workflow does NOT create status labels if they don't exist (instead recommends status in comment).
- The workflow does NOT update issue descriptions directly (only titles if clearly low-quality).
- The workflow respects existing label assignments and avoids churn.

**Alternatives considered:**
- Manual triage: Requires continuous maintainer effort; inconsistent and error-prone.
- Traditional GitHub Actions labels workflow: Limited context reasoning without an AI agent.
- Automated issue closing/updates: Too risky; requires explicit maintainer control.

**Discussion links:** Design based on CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/workflow.md, docs/maintainer-guidelines.md, and AGENTS.md policy files.

### Breaking changes

None. This is a new workflow with no impact on existing code or behavior.

### Special notes for your reviewer

**Policy alignment:**
- Uses repository labels only (no invented labels).
- Maps issue template component/impact dropdowns to `area/*` and `impact/*` labels.
- Respects workflow policy from docs/workflow.md (recommends Discussions for questions).
- Applies UX/accessibility guidance from AGENTS.md for frontend issues.

**Testing:**
- Workflow has been compiled with `gh aw compile --strict` (0 errors, 0 warnings).
- Lock file generated and validated.

### Checklist

- [x] PR: The PR description is expressive and explains rationale and tradeoffs
- [x] Code: Workflow prompt is clear and unambiguous with explicit policy references
- [x] Refactor: Workflow follows gh-aw best practices (conservative, noise-reducing)
- [x] Documentation: New workflow is self-contained; prompt body can be edited without recompilation
- [x] Self-review: Reviewed workflow structure, safe-outputs configuration, and label constraints
- [x] Labels: Applied `type/chore` (automation/CI) and `area/ci` labels

### Release note

```release-note
NONE
```

prs.title: chore(ci): stabilize weekly dependency advisory workflow execution
prs.whatThisPrDoes:
PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): stabilize weekly dependency advisory workflow execution

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- The weekly dependency advisory workflow could fail in non-issue contexts when emitting `update_issue` safe output with default `target: triggering`.
- npm evidence commands (`npm outdated --json`, `npm audit --json`) were interpreted as hard failures when they exited with code 1, even when output was valid for advisory purposes.
- Go runtime used by the workflow was not pinned to the module-required patch version.

After this PR:
- `safe-outputs.update-issue.target` is explicitly set to `"*"`, so updates can run in scheduled/workflow_dispatch contexts.
- npm evidence collection now allows expected non-zero exit code 1 via `run_json_allow_rc` for `outdated` and `audit`.
- Workflow runtime pins Go to `1.26.1`, and compiled lock is regenerated accordingly.

Fixes # N/A
prs.body:
### What this PR does

PR title reminder (non-blocking): use a short, imperative, specific title. With squash merge, this title becomes the merge commit subject.

Optional maintainer hint for squash subject:

Suggested squash subject: chore(ci): stabilize weekly dependency advisory workflow execution

Reference: [docs/maintainer-guidelines.md](docs/maintainer-guidelines.md)

Before this PR:
- The weekly dependency advisory workflow could fail in non-issue contexts when emitting `update_issue` safe output with default `target: triggering`.
- npm evidence commands (`npm outdated --json`, `npm audit --json`) were interpreted as hard failures when they exited with code 1, even when output was valid for advisory purposes.
- Go runtime used by the workflow was not pinned to the module-required patch version.

After this PR:
- `safe-outputs.update-issue.target` is explicitly set to `"*"`, so updates can run in scheduled/workflow_dispatch contexts.
- npm evidence collection now allows expected non-zero exit code 1 via `run_json_allow_rc` for `outdated` and `audit`.
- Workflow runtime pins Go to `1.26.1`, and compiled lock is regenerated accordingly.

Fixes # N/A

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The following tradeoffs were made:
- Used narrow handling for known npm exit-code behavior instead of globally relaxing failures.
- Applied a safe-outputs target fix in frontmatter rather than changing downstream agent behavior.

The following alternatives were considered:
- Wrapping npm commands with `|| true`, but that obscures real failures.
- Keeping `target: triggering` and constraining trigger types, but that would reduce workflow flexibility.

Links to places where the discussion took place: N/A

### Breaking changes

If this PR introduces breaking changes, please describe the changes and the impact on users.
N/A

### Special notes for your reviewer

- Changes are limited to workflow source and compiled lock artifacts.
- No production runtime code paths in server/agent/frontend are modified.

### Labels

Please ensure this PR has:

- exactly one `type/*` label
- one or more `area/*` labels when relevant
- optional `impact/*` labels when they help release context

Type label guidance:

- choose the label that best matches the main reason this PR exists
- if a feature PR also includes docs/tests/refactors, keep `type/feature`
- if the main goal is efficiency, prefer `type/performance` over `type/refactor`

Reference: [docs/release-workflow-plan.md](docs/release-workflow-plan.md)

### Checklist

This checklist is not enforcing, but it's a reminder of items that could be relevant to every PR.
Approvers are expected to review this list.

- [x] PR: The PR description is expressive enough and will help future contributors
- [x] Code: [Write code that humans can understand](https://en.wikiquote.org/wiki/Martin_Fowler#code-for-humans) and [Keep it simple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KISS_principle)
- [x] Refactor: You have [left the code cleaner than you found it (Boy Scout Rule)](https://learning.oreilly.com/library/view/97-things-every/9780596809515/ch08.html)
- [x] Upgrade: Impact of this change on upgrade flows was considered and addressed if required
- [ ] Documentation: A [user-guide update](https://docs.cherry-ai.com) was considered and is present (link) or not required. Check this only when the PR introduces or changes a user-facing feature or behavior.
- [x] Self-review: I have reviewed my own code (e.g., via [`/gh-pr-review`](/.claude/skills/gh-pr-review/SKILL.md), `gh pr diff`, or GitHub UI) before requesting review from others
